original: Jews are expelled from Zurich, Switzerland, on suspicion of poisoning wells
France begins 5th "holy war" against Huguenots
Charles XI becomes King of Sweden (1660-97)
Fire in the Hofburg in Vienna, Emperor Leopold I rescued
Joan Blaeu's publishing house is destroyed by fire in Amsterdam
First major slave rebellion in South America begins, the Berbice uprising in Dutch colony of Bernice (modern Guyana) by slaves protesting conditions
Baron von Steuben joins Continental Army at Valley Forge
Humane Society of Massachusetts incorporates to establish life-saving stations for distressed mariners
First US raw cotton-to-cloth mill is founded in Waltham, Massachusetts
Cato Street conspiracy uncovered, attempt to murder British Prime Minister Earl of Liverpool and Government Ministers
College of Apothecaries organized in Philadephia; 1st US pharmacy college
Boston is incorporated as a city, receiving the first city charter in Massachusetts
Polish revolutionaries march on Kraków, but are defeated
Great Britain & Orange Free state sign Convention of Bloemfontein
Dutch Prime Minister Floris van Hall resigns
Louisiana governor signs public accommodations law
Mississippi is readmitted to US after the Civil War
Major Walter Clopton Wingfield patents a game he calls "sphairistike," now known as lawn tennis
Alabama becomes 1st US state to enact an antitrust law
American Anti-Vivisection Society organized (Philadelphia)
Aluminum manufacturing process developed
The Times of London newspaper publishes the world's first classified ad
French/Italian Riviera struck by Earthquake; 2,000 die
US Congress grants Seal Rocks to San Francisco
1st college student government forms at Bryn Mawr, Pennsylvania
William Heard, AME minister and educator, named American minister to Liberia
Tootsie Roll introduced by Leo Hirshfield
Battle at Hart's Hill, South Africa (Boers vs British army)
Steamer "Rio de Janeiro" sinks in San Francisco Bay
The Sultan agrees to an Austro-Russian plan to reform the government of Macedonia in yet another effort to stifle the rising disorder among the rival Bulgarian
The US and Cuba sign an agreement by which Cuba releases Guantanamo and Bahia Hondo to the US for naval stations
Fiordland National Park in New Zealand created, home to the Milford Track and at 4,600 square miles (12,000 square km), one of the world's largest
Having occupied Korea, Japan signs a treaty with Korea under which it becomes a Japanese protectorate in return for Japanese protection from other powers
United States acquires control of the Panama Canal Zone for $10 million
First Rotary Club formed by 4 men in the Unity Building, Chicago
1st radio contest held (Philadelphia)
Nevada reduces residency requirement for divorces from 1 year to 6 months
US Congress authorizes McKinley Memorial $1 gold coin
First victory of Red Army over the Kaiser's German troops near Narva and Pskov. Since 1923 this date become the Day of Red Army in honour of this victory.
1st US transcontinental air mail flight arrives in NYC from San Francisco
German Republic day with laws against worker
Great Britain lowers import duty on German products from 26% to 5%
Coronation of King Leopold III of Belgium
1st rocket air mail flight, Greenwood Lake, NY
Russian troops conquer Lasi Island
Walt Disney's animated movie "Pinocchio" released
Plutonium first produced and isolated by American chemist Glenn T. Seaborg at Berkeley
Japanese submarine fires on oil refinery in Ellwood, California
Major GeneralOmar Bradleyarrives in Dakar & Marrakesh
Forced deportation of the Chechen and Ingush people to Central Asia.
2nd Dutch government in exile of Pieter Sjoerds Gerbrandy forms in London, London
Canadian troops occupy Kalkar
Operation Grenade: US Lieutenant General Simpson's 9th Army crosses Ruhr
Following his Nordic combined gold medal in St. Moritz (1948), Heikki Hasu takes his 2nd Olympic gold as part of Finland’s 4 x 10k cross country relay team at the Olso Winter Games
Lydia Wideman of Finland becomes first female Olympic cross-country skiing champion; wins inaugural 10k event in Olso; Finnish medal sweep with minors to Mirja Hietamies and Siiri Rantanen
Syrian army drives out president Adib el-Shishakli
Edgar Faure forms French government
20th Congress of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union (CPSU) closes in Moscow, Russia, USSR
The founding congress of the Senegalese Popular Bloc is opened in Dakar
Arturo Frondizi elected President of Argentina
Last Municipal arc light at Mission & 25th removed in San Francisco (installed 1913)
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
KVIE TV channel 6 in Sacramento-Stockton, CA (PBS) begins broadcasting
Carol Heiss wins the women's figure skating gold medal at the Squaw Valley Winter Olympics; US takes the singles double after David Jenkins wins men's event
Demolition begins on Brooklyn's Ebbets Field (opened in 1913)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Charlie Finley signs a 4 year lease to keep MLB A's in Kansas City
Constance Baker Motley elected Manhattan Borough president
Military coup in Syria ends Bitar government
Premier Milton Obote seizes power in Uganda
25th amendment (US Presidential succession) adopted
John Herbert's "Fortune & Men's Eyes" premieres in NYC
Ted Workman replaces Senator Keith Davey as CFL commissioner
11th Daytona 500: LeeRoy Yarbrough driving for Junior Johnson, catches Charlie Glotzbach on final lap to win; first Daytona 500 won on a last lap pass
Groundbreaking TV documentary series "Civilisation" presented by art historian Kenneth Clark premieres on BBC2 in the UK
Nayif Hawatimah forms Dem People's Front for Liberation of Palestine
WWVU (now WNPB) TV channel 24 in Morgantown, WV (PBS) 1st broadcast
Guyana becomes a republic (National Day)
Holy Eucharist given by women for 1st time in Roman Catholic service
Boston Bruins begin 13 NHL game winning streak
US army officer William Calley confesses & implicates Captain Medina during his trial for the My Lai Massacre
Gold goes up $10 overnight to record $95 an ounce in London
In response to the energy crisis, daylight saving time commences nearly two months early in the United States.
Owners announce spring training won't open without a labor contract
Frank Peterson Jr named 1st black general in Marine Corps
George Harrisonreleases "George Harrison" album, featuring the single "Blow Away"
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Oil tanker explosion off Pilos, Greece, causes 37-mil-gallon spillage
Olympic giant slalom champion Hanni Wenzel of Liechtenstein wins
People magazine features drug ordeal of Mackenzie & Papa John Phillips
Michael Frayn's "Noises Off" premieres in London
25th Grammy Awards: "Roxanna"and "Toto IV"; Men at Work win
The Spanish Socialist government of Felipe González and Miguel Boyer nationalizes Rumasa, a holding of José María Ruiz Mateos
The United States Environmental Protection Agency announces its intent to buy out and evacuate the dioxin-contaminated community of Times Beach, Missouri
USFL New Jersey Generals sign Heisman Trophy winner Herschel Walker to a then massive 3 year, $5 million deal
Indiana University basketball coach Bob Knight throws a chair onto the court during a game
Nelli Cooman sets world record for 60m indoor (7.00 sec) at European Indoor Championships in Madrid, Spain
Nelli Fiere-Cooman runs world record 60 m indoor (7.00 sec)
Russian Writers Union accepts Boris Pasternak posthumous as member
Supernova 1987A in LMC 1st seen; 1st naked-eye supernova since 1604
Chicago gives Cubs right to install lights & play up to 18 night games
Ian Smith 173* NZ v India, 136 balls, world record for no 9 bat
“Hold On” single released by En Vogue (Billboard Song of the Year 1990)
"The Doors", directed by Oliver Stone and starring Val Kilmer and Meg Ryan, premieres in Los Angeles, California
Greg Haugen wins WBO light-welterweight title in a split decision over Hector "Macho" Camacho at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas
Military coup in Thailand, Premier Choonhaven arrested
NC is 1st NCAA basketball team to win 1,500 games
Cricketer Andy Flower scores 115* on ODI debut as Zimbabwe lose to Sri Lanka
The Socialist Labour Party is founded in the eastern European country of Georgia
XVI Winter Olympic Games close in Albertville, France
Gary Coleman wins $1,280,000 lawsuit against parents for high fees
India complete a 3-0 series drubbing of England
Sacramento Gold Miners admitted as CFL's 9th franchise (1st US team)
Indians owner Richard Jacobs announces he will pay $10 million to name baseball field (Jacobs Field) at Gateway (becomes official 3/23)
Antoine Nduwayo appointed Premier of Burundi
Dow Jones Industrial Average closes above 4,000 for 1st time (4,003.33)
Mark Waugh scores 130 in World Cup vs Kenya, 207 w/brother Steve
Rajindra Dhanraj takes 9-97 for Trinidad against Leeward Islands
Ali Abu Kamal opens fire in Empire State Building & kills 1
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Charleston, South Carolina on WAVF 96.1 FM
Tornadoes in Florida kills at least 31
US Supreme Court lets Megan's Law (sex offenders information) stand
An avalanche destroys the Austrian village of Galtür, killing 31
British TV drama "Queer as Folk" written by Russell T. Davies, starring Aidan Gillen, Craig Kelly and Charlie Hunnam premieres on Channel 4
Kurdish rebel leader Abdullah Öcalan is charged with treason in Ankara, Turkey
Sky Disc of Nebra, world's oldest map of the stars at 3,600 years old, recovered in police sting operation in Basal, Switzerland after being illegally excavated in Germany
Vote of the controversial French law on colonialism, repealed start of 2006
Dubai Ports World agrees to postpone its plans to take over management of six U.S. ports after the proposal ignited harsh bipartisan criticism on Capitol Hill.
Venezuela's economy grew 0.6% in the last quarter of 2010, technically leaving the recession after six quarters, it is reported today
A series of bomb attacks across 12 Iraqi cities kills 60 and injures 200
51 people are killed and 62 are injured in conflict between rival tribes in Darfur, Sudan
56th Daytona 500: Dale Earnhardt Jr. breaks a 55-race winless streak to win his 2nd Great American Race
A pro-Euromaidan rally is held in Simferopol, Ukraine, while in Kerch, protesters attempt to replace the Ukrainian flag from city hall with a Russian flag
Russia sweeps the medals in the men's 50k cross country skiing at the Sochi Winter Olympics; Alexander Legkov wins gold ahead of teammates Maxim Vylegzhanin and Ilia Chernousov
XXII Winter Olympic Games close in Sochi, Russia
Dutch speed skater Kjeld Nuis wins his second gold medal of the Pyeongchang Winter Olympics in the 1,000m; also 1,500m champion
Russian figure skater Alina Zagitova wins the women's singles gold medal at the Pyeongchang Winter Olympics; earlier sets a new ISU best short program score of 82.92
Study suggests earliest European Palaeolithic artwork in Spain made by Neanderthals not humans in "Science Journal"
Clashes on Venezuelan border kills two as its country's officials try to stop aid getting in
Roy Hodgson overtakes Sir Bobby Robson as the oldest man to manage a Premier League football club when at 71 years, 198 days his Crystal Palace beats Leicester City, 4-1
Sri Lanka becomes first Asian cricket team to clinch a Test series in South Africa chasing down 197 in the 2nd Test in Port Elizabeth; Kusal Mendis 84no, Oshada Fernando 75no
African American Ahmaud Arbery shot to death after being chased down by two white men in Brunswick, Georgia. Video of the killing emerges in May, prompting arrests.
Fashion designer heavyweights Miuccia Prada and Raf Simons announce they will work alongside each other at Prada
First major COVID-19 outbreak in Europe in Italy with 152 cases and three deaths, prompting emergency measures, locking down 10 towns in Lombardy
Violent protests against new citizenship laws in Delhi, India erupt leaving at least 38 people dead over the next few days
COVID-19 antibodies survey in Nigeria suggests 4 million people have had the virus in Lagos state alone, more than officially recorded for whole of Africa
Discovery of oldest Australian rock art, a 17,300-year-old painting of a kangaroo in Kimberley region, Western Australia, published in "Nature Human Behaviour"
German parliament votes to legalize cannabis 407 votes to 226, with restrictions
Johannes Gutenbergprints his first Bible [estimated date]
Francisco Vázquez de Coronado's expedition sets off from Mexico in search of the Seven Cities of Gold (Cibola)
Dutch princeWilliam IIIproclaimed King of England
EngineerJames Watt's patent for a rotary motion for the steam engine (his sun-and-planet gear) is granted
Battle of Buena Vista, Mexico;Zachary Taylorleads mostly volunteer troops against larger Mexican Army force; Mexico retreats, both sides claim victory
Jaap Edenskates world record 10km (17:56)
Émile Zolais imprisoned in France for writing his "J'accuse" letter which accused the government of anti-Semitism and of wrongly jailingAlfred Dreyfus
Tommy Burnsbeats Marvin Hart in 20 for heavyweight boxing title
Russian TsarNicholas IIdissolves Finnish Diet
George Bernard Shaw's play "Misalliance" premieres in London

11th Academy Awards: "You Can’t Take It With You",Spencer Tracy&Bette Daviswins
Lou Theszbeats Everett Marshall in St Louis, to win the National Wrestling Association World Heavyweight C'ship for a 2nd time
US GeneralEisenhoweropens drive to raise $170M in aid for European Jews

Italian tenorLuciano Pavarottimakes his debut at the Vienna State Opera inGiuseppe Verdi's "La Traviata"
6th Daytona 500: Race winnerRichard Pettyleads for record 184 of the 200 laps
Third appearance by the Beatles on "TheEd SullivanShow", featuring performances of three songs taped on February 9, prior to their live television debut
Noam Chomsky's anti-Vietnam war essay "The Responsibility of Intellectuals" is published by the New York Review of Books
Wilt Chamberlainbecomes the first NBA player to score 25,000 points
George Harrisonis fined for speeding and his driving license is suspended for 1 year
Teri Garrappears on "TheBob NewhartShow" in the episode "Confessions of an Orthodontist"
Final meeting between Chinese Communist party ChairmanMao Zedongand former US PresidentRichard Nixon, in Beijing, China
20th Grammy Awards: "Hotel California" by The Eagles;Fleetwood Mac; Debbie Boone win
American speed skaterEric Heidenwins Olympic 10,000m in world record 14:28.13; sweeps all 5 speed skating events at Lake Placid; only athlete to win 5 gold medals at one Winter Games
Soviet cross-country skierNikolay Zimyatovwins his 3rd gold medal of the Lake Placid Winter Olympics when he takes out the 50k event; also wins 30k and relay gold
"Tejerazo" Spanish coup led by Civil Guard officer Lieutenant Colonel Antonio Tejero Molina fails after televised speech by KingJuan Carlos I
GoaltenderPatrick Roymakes his NHL debut for the Montreal Canadiens
US Senate confirmsEdwin Meeseas the 75th Attorney General
Despite losing, Boston Red SoxWade Boggsgets largest arbitration in MLB history, $1.35M
Yvonne van Gennipskates female record 3k (4:11.94)

"Trainspotting" directed byDanny Boylebased on the book by Irvine Welsh, starringEwan McGregor,Robert Carlyleand Jonny Lee Miller opens in cinemas in the UK and Ireland
NBC TV shows "Schindler's List" completely uncensored, and without commercial interruptions; 65M watch
Osama bin Ladenpublishes a fatwa declaring jihad against all Jews and Crusaders

42nd Grammy Awards: "Smooth" by Santana;Christina Aguilerawin
45th Grammy Awards: "Don't Know Why" byNorah Joneswins
Slovakia Summit 2005 begins;George W. Bushand Russian PresidentVladimir Putinattend - 1st visit of a sitting US President to Slovakia
Danielle Steel’s novel “Big Girl” is published
Canada wins theOlympic ice hockeydouble with a 3-0 victory over Sweden in the men's gold medal game in Sochi; first team to finish the tournament undefeated since 1984
Argentine soccer starLionel Messiscores the 50th hat-trick of his career as Barcelona beats Sevilla, 4-2 in the Spanish La Liga
Nigerian presidential election: PresidentMuhammadu Buhariwins re-election defeating Atiku Abubakar, amid violence and a delay, on turnout of 35.6%
Sudan's PresidentOmar al-Bashirdeclares a national emergency, dismisses the federal government and sacks all state governors
China's Supreme LeaderXi Jinpingdescribes the country's COVID-19 outbreak as the China's largest health emergency since 1949
PresidentMoon Jae-inputs South Korea on high alert, empowering the government to lock down cities after 602 cases of COVID-19 reported with six deaths
Tiger Woodscrashes his car driving south of Los Angeles, injuring both his legs
Former Hollywood executiveHarvey Weinsteinsentenced to 16 years in prison for rape in Los Angeles, on top of the 23 years he is already serving for sex crimes
German Elections:Friedrich Merz's center-right Christian Democratic Union wins the most votes with 28.6%, far-right Alternative for Germany wins their largest-ever share with 20.8%
The Alamo is besieged for 13 days until March 6 by the Mexican army under GeneralSanta Anna; the entire garrison is eventually killed
Battle of Eniwetok ends as US forces complete the capture of Parry Island from the Japanese 1st Amphibious Brigade
US Marines raise the flag of the United States on top of Mt. Suribachi, Iwo Jima. Pulitzer Prize-winning photo by Joe Rosenthal later becomes iconic, inspiring the Marine Corps War Memorial sculpture
First mass inoculation against polio with theJonas Salkvaccine takes place at Arsenal Elementary School in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
